widgets_values:
SONIC LIP-SYNC RENDERER (Head + Shoulders)
=========================================

USE THIS WORKFLOW FOR: scenes where the singer is on camera
                          (verses in our shot-list strategy)
EXPECTED SPEED: ~2 min per 10s clip on RTX 4090 mobile (16GB)

WIDGET NOTES (SonicModelLoader):
- [0] model file (sonic_512.safetensors — Tencent Sonic)
- [1] precision: 'fp16' (recommended for 16GB VRAM)
- [2] device: 'cuda'
- [3] use_compile: true (torch.compile — ~30% speedup after warmup)

WIDGET NOTES (SonicSampler):
- [0] width: 512 (head+shoulders framing; 768 for tighter face)
- [1] height: 512
- [2] steps: 25 (Sonic converges fast; 20-30 range)
- [3] schedule: 'fixed' (or 'karras' for slightly smoother)
- [4] motion_strength: 0.6 (0.3=static, 1.0=very animated head)
- [5] lip_sync_strength: 0.7 (0.5=loose, 1.0=tight)
- [6] keep_identity: true (preserve singer's face from ref)
- [7] animate_background: true (subtle parallax background motion)
- [8] audio_weight: 1.0 (how much audio drives mouth)
- [9] pose_weight: 1.0 (head pose weight)
- [10] seed

AUDIO INPUT:
  • Use the ISOLATED VOCALS from Demucs (vocal_segment_verse1.wav)
  • NOT the full Suno mix — that would make the mouth move during instrumental parts
  • batch_render_lipsync.py trims vocal_segment_NNN.wav per scene automatically

IMAGE INPUT:
  • Single singer reference image (singer_ref_01.png)
  • Recommended: front-facing portrait, head + shoulders visible, neutral expression
  • Resolution: at least 768×768 for crisp face detail
  • Avoid heavy shadows on face — Sonic needs clear facial features

16GB VRAM TIPS:
  • Sonic 512×512 needs ~8GB peak → very safe on your hardware
  • 768×768 needs ~12GB → still safe
  • 1024×1024 needs ~16GB → risky, use --lowvram
  • If you see face identity drift, lower motion_strength to 0.4-0.5

WHY SONIC OVER OTHERS:
  • Best lip-sync accuracy in our testing (vs SadTalker, Hallo2)
  • Natural head micro-motion (doesn't look robotic)
  • Keeps background subtle (not frozen solid)
  • Audio-driven only (no need to pre-generate head pose sequences)

KNOWN LIMITATIONS:
  • Can drift identity on very long clips (>30s) — batch_render caps at 20s per scene
  • Sometimes produces artifacts at extreme expressions (screaming, laughing)
  • Doesn't handle glasses/hats well — use clean portrait if possible

groups.title: Inputs: singer ref image + isolated vocal audio

metadata.title: Lip-Sync Renderer - Sonic (Audio-Driven Portrait)
metadata.author: comfyui-mv-lipsync-workflow pack
metadata.description: Generates a talking-head video of the singer, lip-synced to an isolated vocal segment. Used for verse scenes where the singer is on camera.
required_custom_nodes: ComfyUI-Sonic (Tencent Sonic ComfyUI wrapper — search ComfyUI-Manager for 'Sonic')
metadata.16gb_vram_note: Sonic 512x512 + fp16 = ~8GB peak VRAM. Very safe on RTX 4090 mobile. 768x768 also fits comfortably (~12GB).
